UiKitComponentStorybook.keywords: uikit, storybook, hierarchy, play-function, a11y-audit, ai-generated, spec-mirror

BeliefState.Axiom:
The Storybook sidebar hierarchy is a direct mirror of the spec's Variants table structure. For Button, the hierarchy is:
``` Button / Appearance / Mode â”śâ”€â”€ Accent â”‚ â”śâ”€â”€ Primary â”‚ â”‚ â”śâ”€â”€ Small Normal â”‚ â”‚ â”śâ”€â”€ Small Hover â”‚ â”‚ â”śâ”€â”€ Small Active â”‚ â”‚ â”śâ”€â”€ Small Disabled â”‚ â”‚ â”śâ”€â”€ Medium Normal â”‚ â”‚ â””â”€â”€ ... â”‚ â”śâ”€â”€ Secondary â”‚ â”śâ”€â”€ Tertiary â”‚ â””â”€â”€ Outline â”śâ”€â”€ Neutral â””â”€â”€ Contrary ```
Title format: `ComponentName/Appearance/Mode` (e.g. `Button/Accent/Primary`). The spec's Variants table is the single source of truth for the hierarchy â€” every `(appearance, mode, size, state)` combination maps to exactly one story. No "convenience" groupings that collapse size or state, no ad-hoc categories.
The `title` field in `export default` sets the hierarchy root: `title: 'Button/Accent/Primary'` groups all stories for that appearance+mode under that sidebar node. Each sizeĂ—state combination becomes a named export within that group.

The story title is derived from the spec's component name and variant hierarchy, not invented:
For Button, variants are grouped by `(appearance, mode)`. The story file organizes stories into title groups matching this grouping. A single `.stories.ts` file can export multiple `meta` objects (using named exports) to group stories under different titles, OR a single file groups all stories under a common root and uses story names for differentiation.
Preferred pattern for uikit: one `.stories.ts` file per component. Stories are organized by exporting a single `meta` with a root title like `'Button'`, and story names encode the variant: `AccentPrimarySmallNormal`, `AccentPrimarySmallHover`, etc. The Storybook sidebar auto-groups by title segments.
Alternative (used in Button spec examples): multiple `export default` in separate files, or named exports with different titles. Either pattern is acceptable as long as the hierarchy matches the spec.

Definitions.Definition: The Storybook sidebar tree that is a 1:1 reflection of the spec's Variants table. Hierarchy format: `ComponentName / Appearance / Mode`. Every leaf is a `Size Ă— State` story.

The mandatory `play` function in every story that asserts: (1) correct ARIA role via `getByRole`, (2) correct `data-state` attribute via `toHaveAttribute`, (3) a11y violations via axe-core parameters. Combined, these three assertions form the executable component contract.

Pattern.Intent: Complete canonical `.stories.ts` file for a uikit Button component.
Pattern.Why: One file per component; `meta` with `ai-generated` tag and a11y error-level; stories compose from base; every story has `play` with `getByRole` and `toHaveAttribute`; variant params are explicit; kebab-case import path.

Pattern.Intent: Story with visual style assertion for state-dependent rendering.
Pattern.Why: Adds `toHaveStyle` assertion on top of the role+attribute baseline. Background color values come from the spec's BDD scenarios (hex â†’ rgb conversion for jsdom). Only add when the spec specifies an exact color; don't fabricate colors.

AntiPattern.Bad: Story title `'Components/Button'` or `'UI/Button/Primary'` â€” hierarchy invented by developer.
AntiPattern.WhyBad: Hierarchy not derived from spec (`AX_HIERARCHY_MIRRORS_SPEC`). When the spec adds a new appearance, the developer must manually reorganize stories. The spec and Storybook drift apart silently.
AntiPattern.Good: Title `'Button/Accent/Primary'` â€” matches spec's Variants table columns. Adding a new appearance is a mechanical addition of one title group.

AntiPattern.WhyBad: Play function required by `AX_PLAY_FUNCTION_REQUIRED`. Without `play`, `run-story-tests` passes trivially without asserting any contract. The story is documentation, not an executable spec mirror.
AntiPattern.Good: Every story has `play` with `getByRole` + `toHaveAttribute('data-state', ...)` at minimum.

AntiPattern.Bad: Story or meta lacks `parameters: { a11y: { test: 'error' } }`.
AntiPattern.WhyBad: A11y audit not configured (`AX_A11Y_AUDIT_PARAMETERS`). axe-core does not run during `run-story-tests`; a11y violations (missing labels, wrong contrast, invalid roles) pass silently.
AntiPattern.Good: Add `parameters: { a11y: { test: 'error' } }` to `meta` (applies to all stories) or to each individual story.

AntiPattern.Bad: `SmallHover` story restates all args from `SmallNormal` instead of composing.
AntiPattern.WhyBad: Args restated instead of composed (`AX_COMPOSE_FROM_BASE_STORY`). When `SmallNormal` gains a new prop, `SmallHover` silently falls behind without any test pointing at the drift.
AntiPattern.Good: `args: { ...SmallNormal.args, state: 'hover' }` â€” composition makes the diff visible.

AntiPattern.Bad: One story named `AccentPrimaryAllSizes` with `play` that loops over sizes and asserts all of them.
AntiPattern.WhyBad: Multiple concepts in one story. When a test fails, the failure message says which size broke but the story name doesn't identify which variant the story was testing. The spec demands one story per variant.

AntiPattern.Bad: Failing story is marked `.skip` or deleted to make `run-story-tests` green.
AntiPattern.WhyBad: Failure silenced instead of fixed. The story was the canary for a real bug â€” skipping it buries the bug. The self-verification loop is now blind to that variant.
AntiPattern.Good: Fix the component or the story, re-run until green. If the failure is environment-dependent, surface as a blocker with deferred-ownership reference.

Hook.Purpose: Run all story tests for the component file. Green run = stories pass type-check, render, and their `play` assertions succeed.
Hook.Expected: Exit 0; all stories pass. Non-zero requires fix or explicit deferred-ownership reference.

Hook.Purpose: Verify every exported story has a `play` function.
Hook.Command: find src/ui -name '*.stories.ts' -not -path '*/node_modules/*' -print0 | xargs -0 grep -l 'play:' || echo MISSING_PLAY
Hook.Expected: Every `.stories.ts` file must appear. `MISSING_PLAY` means some file has no `play` function.

Hook.Purpose: Verify a11y audit is configured at `error` level.
Hook.Command: find src/ui -name '*.stories.ts' -not -path '*/node_modules/*' -print0 | xargs -0 grep -l "a11y.*test.*error" || echo MISSING_A11Y
Hook.Expected: Every `.stories.ts` file must appear. `MISSING_A11Y` means a11y audit is not configured.

Hook.Purpose: Verify 'ai-generated' tag is present in every stories file.
Hook.Command: find src/ui -name '*.stories.ts' -not -path '*/node_modules/*' -print0 | xargs -0 grep -l "'ai-generated'" || echo MISSING_TAG
Hook.Expected: Every `.stories.ts` file must appear. `MISSING_TAG` means the reserved tag is absent.

UiKitComponentStorybook.RewardCriteria:
âś… Storybook hierarchy is a 1:1 mirror of the spec's Variants table â€” `ComponentName / Appearance / Mode`. âś… One story per variant combination (size Ă— state); story name = ``. âś… Every story has a `play` function asserting role via `getByRole` and state via `toHaveAttribute('data-state', ...)`. âś… A11y audit enabled at `error` level for all stories. âś… `'ai-generated'` tag present on `meta` or on each agent-authored story. âś… New stories compose args from the closest base story. âś… Imports follow canonical order: Storybook types â†’ test utilities â†’ component. âś… All stories pass `run-story-tests` before handoff.
âťŚ Hierarchy invented by developer (not matching spec). âťŚ Story without `play` function. âťŚ Missing a11y audit configuration. âťŚ Missing `'ai-generated'` tag. âťŚ Args restated instead of composed. âťŚ Multiple variants collapsed into one story. âťŚ Failing story skipped or deleted instead of fixed. âťŚ `run-story-tests` not executed before handoff.
